mission.title: Install and Configure Kubean on Kubernetes
mission.description: Kubean is a production-ready cluster lifecycle management toolchain based on Kubespray, designed to simplify the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters. Installing Kubean allows you to leverage its powerful lifecycle management features for your Kubernetes environments.

steps.title: Add the Kubean Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Kubean Helm chart repository to your Helm client:
```bash
helm repo add kubean-io https://kubean-io.github.io/kubean-helm-chart/
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Kubean using Helm
steps.description:
Install Kubean in the `kubean-system` namespace:
```bash
helm install kubean kubean-io/kubean --create-namespace -n kubean-system
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Kubean operator pod is running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubean-system
helm ls -n kubean-system
```

steps.title: Verify CRDs
steps.description:
Confirm that the Kubean Custom Resource Definitions are registered:
```bash
kubectl get crd | grep kubean.io
```
You should see clusters, clusteroperations, localartifactsets, and manifests CRDs.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Kubean will show the operator pod running in the kubean-system namespace with all CRDs registered. You can then define Cluster and ClusterOperation resources to manage Kubernetes cluster lifecycles.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kubean-io https://kubean-io.github.io/kubean-helm-chart/
helm repo update

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Kubean release and clean up:
```bash
helm uninstall kubean -n kubean-system
kubectl get crd -o name | grep kubean.io | xargs kubectl delete
kubectl delete namespace kubean-system
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kubean
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade kubean kubean-io/kubean -n kubean-system
kubectl get pods -n kubean-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Operator pod not starting
troubleshooting.description:
Check the operator pod logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs -n kubean-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubean --tail=50
```

troubleshooting.title: CRDs not registered
troubleshooting.description:
Verify the Helm release installed correctly:
```bash
helm ls -n kubean-system
kubectl get crd | grep kubean.io
```

prerequisites.description: Requires a Kubernetes cluster and Helm 3 installed.
